items.title: The NBA isn't just instituting draft lottery reform in 2027, it is also putting in 2nd round reform. Next year, the top 16 picks of the second rounds will be in reverse order of the lottery. So if a team draws the 16th pick in the 2027 draft, it will get to pick first in the 2nd
items.body_excerpt: The NBA isn't just instituting draft lottery reform in 2027, it is also putting in 2nd round reform. Next year, the top 16 picks of the second rounds will be in reverse order of the lottery. So if a team draws the 16th pick in the 2027 draft, it will get to pick first in the 2nd round.

items.title: Suns GM Brian Gregory on Koa Peat's 3-point shooting: "Obviously that was a topic, and he's a guy that I think is going to develop into a very good shooter."

Suns GM Brian Gregory on Koa Peat's 3-point shooting: "Obviously that was a topic, and he's a guy that I think is going to develop into a very good shooter."

Cited his work ethic and guys needing to develop certain areas of their game at 19, but called his ability to get to the rim "critical"

items.body_excerpt:
Brian Gregory on Koa Peat being the hometown pick for the Suns: "It's different when this is where you grew up."

items.title: The Timebrwolves will need to fold the LaMelo Ball trade with Nets/Bulls trade since their post-trade payroll is above the first apron.

The Timebrwolves will need to fold the LaMelo Ball trade with Nets/Bulls trade since their post-trade payroll is above the first apron.

Julius Randle & Naz Reid for LaMelo Ball & Josh Green is a 100 percent salary match.

They're now hard capped to second apron for aggregation.

items.title: Two paths for the Hornets post-LaMelo Ball trade:
items.body_excerpt:
Two paths for the Hornets post-LaMelo Ball trade:

1. Stay over the cap and keep a massive $40.7M TPE (most likely)

2. Renounce FAs and create $43M in cap space this offseason (less likely, as re-signing C. White would have to come out of cap space and would eliminate the TPE)

-Plays bball like a 10 y/o

-Locked in for 3 yrs

items.title: LaMelo Ball 2025-26 Shot Map
items.body_excerpt:
LaMelo Ball 2025-26 Shot Map

Red = above average
Blue = below average 

 —> courtsketch.com/shotmap

images.alt:
Shot chart for LaMelo Ball, 2025-26 NBA regular season. Overall eFG%: 51.6 on 1,244 shots. Color indicates efficiency relative to league average — blue is below average, red is above average. Hexagon size indicates shot frequency.

**Zone breakdown by frequency and FG%:**
Three-pointers (>24 ft) account for 59.5% of his shots at 36.8% FG%. Mid-range (16-24 ft) is nearly non-existent at 2.3% frequency, 46.4% FG%. Short mid-range (8-16 ft) is 13.3% of shots at 38.0%. Rim attempts (<8 ft) make up 24.9% of shots at 51.0%.

**Directional 3PT breakdown:**
Left wing: 37.0%. Left corner: 34.9%. Top of arc (center): 35.8%. Right wing: 35.1%. Right corner: 50.0% (3 shots). Far left: 42.1%. Far right: 43.1%.

**Paint zones:**
Short mid paint: 36.6%. Restricted area: 51.0%. Left elbow: 62.5% (small sample).

Chart styled by @ofilispeaks, automation by @GabeLeftBrain, original design by @kirkgoldsberry. Via courtsketch.com/shotmap.

items.title: Naz Reid 2025-26 Shot Map
items.body_excerpt:
Naz Reid 2025-26 Shot Map

Red = above average
Blue = below average 

 —> courtsketch.com/shotmap

images.alt:
Shot chart for Naz Reid, 2025-26 NBA regular season. Overall eFG%: 54.9 on 871 shots. Color indicates efficiency relative to league average — blue is below average, red is above average. Hexagon size indicates shot frequency.

**Zone breakdown by frequency and FG%:**
Three-pointers (>24 ft) account for 51.4% of his shots at 36.2% FG%. Mid-range (16-24 ft) is nearly nonexistent at 0.3% frequency, 33.3% FG%. Short mid-range (8-16 ft) is 12.9% of shots at 40.2%. Rim attempts (<8 ft) make up 35.4% of shots at 61.4%.

**Directional 3PT breakdown:**
Far left: 40.6%. Left corner: 34.3%. Left wing: 34.1%. Top of arc: 31.3%. Right wing: 34.9%. Right corner: 34.5%. Far right: 42.3%.

**Paint zones:**
Short mid paint: 47.9%. Restricted area: 61.4%.

**Notable small samples:**
1 shot logged on the left mid-range extended area. 2 shots on the right wing extended area.

Chart styled by @ofilispeaks, automation by @GabeLeftBrain, original design by @kirkgoldsberry. Via courtsketch.com/shotmap.

Red = above average
Blue = below average

images.alt:
Shot chart for OG Anunoby, 2025-26 NBA Postseason. 198 total shots. Overall eFG%: 67.2.

Court zones, frequency, and FG%:

Rim (under 8 feet): 41.9% of shots, 66.3% FG. Paint/floater (8-16 feet): 8.6% of shots, 41.2% FG. Midrange (16-24 feet): 4.0% of shots, 62.5% FG. Three-pointers (beyond 24 feet): 45.5% of shots, 48.9% FG.

Zone-by-zone 3PT breakdown: Left corner 42.9%, left wing 42.1%, top of key 55.6%, right wing 42.9%, right corner 59.3%.

Most hexagons are red (above league average), with scattered blue and teal hexagons (below average) primarily in the short midrange and floater zones. Shot volume is highest at the rim and along the 3PT arc.

items.title: There is some devastating news from the college sports world to share at this late hour: Longtime Louisville SID Kenny Klein, who "retired" in 2022 but never left the scene in the years since, has died at 65.
items.body_excerpt:
There is some devastating news from the college sports world to share at this late hour: Longtime Louisville SID Kenny Klein, who "retired" in 2022 but never left the scene in the years since, has died at 65.

There isn't a more respected person in the college sports space. Not one. A tragedy.

media.title: Kenny Klein, former longtime Louisville Athletics administrator, dies
media.description: Kenny Klein, a former Louisville Athletics administrator of 39 years who worked more than 1,300 straight men's basketball games, has died at age 66.

items.title: Hollander POTD Jerome Kersey. Longwood SF drafted 46th by POR in 84. Started out as raw bench guy but became starter in 87-88 & had 19.2 ppg. Started for 6 yrs for 90s POR. From 95-01 was bench guy for 6 teams. Won ring w/SA in 98-99. Per bio "plays w/reckless abandon." RIP.

items.title: How the Pistons acquire Isaiah Joe is TBD.
items.body_excerpt:
How the Pistons acquire Isaiah Joe is TBD.

They could use their $15 million tax mid-level or expand the Isaiah Stewart trade to acquire him.

They could also still create as much as $34 million in cap space to absorb Joe while retaining Jalen Duren's Bird rights.

This is without anything on the books for Lu Dort, Isaiah Hartenstein or Kenrich Williams. OKC creating 2A room.

items.title: It is true that OKC is cutting costs. Literally a fact. We'll see if any other moves are made to further reduce costs, which seems likely.
items.body_excerpt:
It is true that OKC is cutting costs. Literally a fact. We'll see if any other moves are made to further reduce costs, which seems likely.

But usually when teams dump salary, they are filling those roster spots with vet minimums or fringe 2nd rounders. OKC is filling with 1st round draft talent.

items.title: If Jose Alvarado's new deal starts at the same $4.5M PO he's declining, and includes 8% raises, it should look like this:
items.body_excerpt:
If Jose Alvarado's new deal starts at the same $4.5M PO he's declining, and includes 8% raises, it should look like this:
26-27: $4.5M
27-28: $4.9M
28-29: $5.2M

Doesn't really change the projection much for the tax or aprons. Somewhere around $14M under the second apron with five open roster spots.

items.title: June 26, 1996: With the fourth pick in the NBA Draft, the Milwaukee Bucks select Stephon Marbury from Georgia Tech.
items.body_excerpt:
June 26, 1996: With the fourth pick in the NBA Draft, the Milwaukee Bucks select Stephon Marbury from Georgia Tech.

Less than an hour later, it was announced that Marbury had been traded to the Minnesota Timberwolves for Ray Allen and a first round pick in the 1998 NBA Draft.
